     3	# IssuableFinder
     4	#
     5	# Used to filter Issues and MergeRequests collections by set of params
     6	#
     7	# Note: This class is NOT meant to be instantiated. Instead you should
     8	#       look at IssuesFinder or EpicsFinder, which inherit from this.
     9	#
    10	# Arguments:
    11	#   klass - actual class like Issue or MergeRequest
    12	#   current_user - which user use
    13	#   params:
    14	#     scope: 'created_by_me' or 'assigned_to_me' or 'all'
    15	#     state: 'opened' or 'closed' or 'locked' or 'all'
    16	#     group_id: integer
    17	#     project_id: integer
    18	#     milestone_title: string (cannot be simultaneously used with milestone_wildcard_id)
    19	#     milestone_wildcard_id: 'none', 'any', 'upcoming', 'started' (cannot be simultaneously used with milestone_title)
    20	#     release_tag: string
    21	#     author_id: integer
    22	#     author_username: username string or a group handle (e.g., '@group/subgroup')
    23	#     assignee_id: integer or 'None' or 'Any'
    24	#     closed_by_id: integer
    25	#     assignee_username: string
    26	#     search: string
    27	#     in: 'title', 'description', or a string joining them with comma
    28	#     label_name: string
    29	#     sort: string
    30	#     non_archived: boolean
    31	#     iids: integer[]
    32	#     my_reaction_emoji: string
    33	#     created_after: datetime
    34	#     created_before: datetime
    35	#     updated_after: datetime
    36	#     updated_before: datetime
    37	#     attempt_group_search_optimizations: boolean
    38	#     attempt_project_search_optimizations: boolean
    39	#     crm_contact_id: integer
    40	#     crm_organization_id: integer
    41	#
    42	class IssuableFinder
    43	  prepend FinderWithCrossProjectAccess
    44	  include FinderMethods
    45	  include CreatedAtFilter
    46	  include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
    47	  include UpdatedAtFilter
    48	
    49	  requires_cross_project_access unless: -> { params.project? }
    50	
    51	  FULL_TEXT_SEARCH_TERM_PATTERN = '[\u0000-\u02FF\u1E00-\u1EFF\u2070-\u218F]*'
    52	  FULL_TEXT_SEARCH_TERM_REGEX = /\A#{FULL_TEXT_SEARCH_TERM_PATTERN}\z/
    53	  NEGATABLE_PARAMS_HELPER_KEYS = %i[project_id scope status include_subgroups].freeze
    54	
    55	  attr_accessor :current_user, :params
    56	  attr_reader :original_params
    57	  attr_writer :parent
    58	
    59	  delegate(*%i[milestones], to: :params)
    60	
    61	  class << self
    62	    def scalar_params
    63	      @scalar_params ||= %i[
    64	        assignee_id
    65	        closed_by_id
    66	        assignee_username
    67	        author_id
    68	        author_username
    69	        crm_contact_id
    70	        crm_organization_id
    71	        in
    72	        label_name
    73	        milestone_title
    74	        release_tag
    75	        my_reaction_emoji
    76	        search
    77	        subscribed
    78	      ]
    79	    end
    80	
    81	    def array_params
    82	      @array_params ||= { label_name: [], assignee_username: [] }
    83	    end
    84	
    85	    # This should not be used in controller strong params!
    86	    def negatable_scalar_params
    87	      @negatable_scalar_params ||= scalar_params - %i[search in]
    88	    end
    89	
    90	    # This should not be used in controller strong params!
    91	    def negatable_array_params
    92	      @negatable_array_params ||= array_params.keys.append(:iids)
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    # This should not be used in controller strong params!
    96	    def negatable_params
    97	      @negatable_params ||= negatable_scalar_params + negatable_array_params
    98	    end
    99	
   100	    def valid_params
   101	      @valid_params ||= scalar_params + [array_params.merge(or: {}, not: {})]
   102	    end
   103	  end
   104	
   105	  def params_class
   106	    IssuableFinder::Params
   107	  end
   108	
   109	  def klass
   110	    raise NotImplementedError
   111	  end
   112	
   113	  def initialize(current_user, params = {})
   114	    @current_user = current_user
   115	    @original_params = params
   116	    @params = params_class.new(params, current_user, klass)
   117	  end
   118	
   119	  def execute
   120	    items = init_collection
   121	    items = filter_items(items)
   122	
   123	    # Let's see if we have to negate anything
   124	    items = filter_negated_items(items) if should_filter_negated_args?
   125	
   126	    # This has to be last as we use a CTE as an optimization fence
   127	    # https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/static/queries-with.html
   128	    items = by_search(items)
   129	
   130	    sort(items)
   131	  end
   132	
   133	  def filter_items(items)
   134	    items = by_parent(items)
   135	    items = by_scope(items)
   136	    items = by_created_at(items)
   137	    items = by_updated_at(items)
   138	    items = by_closed_at(items)
   139	    items = by_state(items)
   140	    items = by_assignee(items)
   141	    items = by_closed_by(items)
   142	    items = by_author(items)
   143	    items = by_non_archived(items)
   144	    items = by_iids(items)
   145	    items = by_milestone(items)
   146	    items = by_release(items)
   147	    items = by_label(items)
   148	    items = by_my_reaction_emoji(items)
   149	    items = by_crm_contact(items)
   150	    items = by_subscribed(items)
   151	    by_crm_organization(items)
   152	  end
   153	
   154	  def should_filter_negated_args?
   155	    # API endpoints send in `nil` values so we test if there are any non-nil
   156	    not_params.present? && not_params.values.any?
   157	  end
   158	
   159	  # Negates all params found in `negatable_params`
   160	  def filter_negated_items(items)
   161	    items = by_negated_milestone(items)
   162	    items = by_negated_release(items)
   163	    items = by_negated_my_reaction_emoji(items)
   164	    by_negated_iids(items)
   165	  end
   166	
   167	  def row_count
   168	    Gitlab::IssuablesCountForState
   169	      .new(self, nil, fast_fail: true)
   170	      .for_state_or_opened(params[:state])
   171	  end
   172	
   173	  # We often get counts for each state by running a query per state, and
   174	  # counting those results. This is typically slower than running one query
   175	  # (even if that query is slower than any of the individual state queries) and
   176	  # grouping and counting within that query.
   177	  #
   178	  # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord
   179	  def count_by_state
   180	    count_params = params.merge(state: nil, sort: nil)
   181	    finder = self.class.new(current_user, count_params)
   182	
   183	    state_counts = finder
   184	      .execute
   185	      .without_order
   186	      .group(:state_id)
   187	      .count
   188	
   189	    counts = Hash.new(0)
   190	
   191	    state_counts.each do |key, value|
   192	      counts[count_key(key)] += value
   193	    end
   194	
   195	    counts[:all] = counts.values.sum
   196	    counts.with_indifferent_access
   197	  end
   198	  # rubocop: enable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord
   199	
   200	  def search
   201	    params[:search].presence
   202	  end
   203	
   204	  def use_cte_for_search?
   205	    strong_memoize(:use_cte_for_search) do
   206	      next false unless search
   207	      next false unless default_or_simple_sort?
   208	
   209	      attempt_group_search_optimizations? || attempt_project_search_optimizations?
   210	    end
   211	  end
   212	
   213	  def parent_param=(obj)
   214	    @parent = obj
   215	    params[parent_param] = parent if parent
   216	  end
   217	
   218	  def parent_param
   219	    case parent
   220	    when Project
   221	      :project_id
   222	    when Group
   223	      :group_id
   224	    else
   225	      raise "Unexpected parent: #{parent.class}"
   226	    end
   227	  end
   228	
   229	  private
   230	
   231	  attr_reader :parent
   232	
   233	  def use_minimum_char_limit?
   234	    !use_cte_for_search?
   235	  end
   236	
   237	  def not_params
   238	    strong_memoize(:not_params) do
   239	      params_class.new(params[:not].dup, current_user, klass).tap do |not_params|
   240	        next unless not_params.present?
   241	
   242	        # These are "helper" params that modify the results, like :in and :search. They usually come in at the top-level
   243	        # params, but if they do come in inside the `:not` params, the inner ones should take precedence.
   244	        not_helpers = params.slice(*NEGATABLE_PARAMS_HELPER_KEYS)
   245	                            .merge(params[:not].to_h.slice(*NEGATABLE_PARAMS_HELPER_KEYS))
   246	        not_helpers.each do |key, value|
   247	          not_params[key] = value unless not_params[key].present?
   248	        end
   249	      end
   250	    end
   251	  end
   252	
   253	  def init_collection
   254	    return klass.all if params.user_can_see_all_issuables?
   255	
   256	    # Only admins and auditors can see hidden issuables, for other users we filter out hidden issuables
   257	    klass.without_hidden
   258	  end

   126	      def handle_merge_request_errors!(merge_request)
   127	        return if merge_request.valid?
   128	
   129	        errors = merge_request.errors
   130	
   131	        UNPROCESSABLE_ERROR_KEYS.each do |error|
   132	          unprocessable_entity!(errors[error]) if errors.has_key?(error)
   133	        end
   134	
   135	        conflict!(errors[:validate_branches]) if errors.has_key?(:validate_branches)
   136	
   137	        render_validation_error!(merge_request)
   138	      end

The guard pattern is layered: finders constrain visible collections, policies define MR/project abilities, REST and GraphQL load through helpers or `authorized_find!`, and services repeat permission-sensitive filtering before mutating metadata. I’m now building a concrete dependency inventory by area from targeted MR references and association owners, then I’ll rank the blast radius.
